name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **THE INSURANCE COST CHANNEL IS THE LIVE PROOF THE PHYSICAL STACK HAS NOT UNWOUND — GULF AWRP ~1% OF HULL (SOME 0.8% POST-NO-CLAIMS-BONUS), ~8x THE PRE-WAR 0.1-0.15% BASELINE; HEADLINE QUOTES STILL RUN 3-8% = $3-8M PER TRANSIT, AND IT IS NOT UNWINDING ('Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge' — S&P Global / Khaleej Times). TODAY'S CONFIRMATION: the SECOND US 'defensive' strike of the ceasefire — the US downed FOUR Iranian drones near Hormuz AND struck an Iranian ground-control station near Bandar Abbas (May 28) — is direct evidence that physical risk is ACTIVE inside the ceasefire, exactly the Croft thesis (insurers/shippers won't rush back into a strait Iran still 'manages'). This matters because today the dominant tape was the OPPOSITE on paper: the IRGC fired one calibrated ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty) and BOTH sides kept negotiating — NOT a return to war — so paper oil de-priced again while insurance stayed elevated. Insurance is the live cost channel even as paper oil de-prices: the cleanest paper-physical non-confirmation of the cycle (C6). Deal hardened around it (Trump 'no sanctions relief,' nuclear 'deadlock,' WH called Iran's state-TV deal draft a 'complete fabrication,' 60-day MOU awaits Trump's signature) — and on a clean signature the physical cost stack still bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not a session; if it breaks, insurance never moved. The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude fired again today (paper de-prices on calibrated/negotiating tape, insurance holds 8x). PRIOR (May 27): **THE PHYSICAL STACK HAS NOT UNWOUND — WAR-RISK INSURANCE STILL ~0.8-1% OF HULL (PEAKS 5%+) vs 0.2% PRE-WAR = MULTIPLES ELEVATED AND NOT UNWINDING — THE CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL NON-CONFIRMATION OF THE CYCLE (C6). The CROFT THESIS VALIDATED IN REAL TIME: the May 25 US strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats (intel A1) show physical risk is ACTIVE EVEN INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE — Iran was still laying the very mines the deal requires it to clear, and the US struck the boats doing it. This is exactly why insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage' (Croft: 'if Iran continues to have the final say on passage, some insurers and shippers may be reluctant to rush back in'). The PAPER side bled this window — the crisis-trade equities unwound (Frontline -3.0%, BOAT -2.0%, Cheniere -10.3% MoM, CF/Nutrien -4-5% MoM, Lockheed -18.8% 3M) on deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation — but the PHYSICAL stack priced ZERO operational change: VLCC war-risk ~0.8-1% (NOT unwinding), VLCC volumes -36% intact, urea >$700/t, TTF +30% YoY, helium +40-100%. THE BIFURCATION: paper equities are selling the deal while physical markets price zero change. Even if the deal signs cleanly, the physical cost stack takes 6-12 weeks to unwind; if it breaks (Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike), these equities gap back up first while physical never moved. The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude (paper whipsaws/de-prices, insurance holds) fired again today.** PRIOR (May 25): **WAR-RISK INSURANCE STILL 3-8% OF HULL VALUE ($3-8M PER TRANSIT) vs 0.25% PRE-WAR = 8x+ ELEVATED AND NOT UNWINDING — THE CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL NON-CONFIRMATION SIGNAL OF THE CYCLE (C4). Today the paper market priced the US-Iran deal as near-done (Brent <$100 / -3.2%, TTF -4.2%, urea -7.4%, India equities +1.3%), but the physical insurance complex priced ZERO operational change. Khaleej Times explicit: 'Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge.' VLCC volumes still -36% through Hormuz (Lloyd's List). The mechanism: insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage' — even on a clean signed deal the physical cost stack takes 6-12 weeks to unwind, so the equity-relief bounce front-runs real relief by weeks. This is the SLOW-unwinding physical floor under the paper relief. HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' — Lloyd's JWC has NOT added Eastern Med or Haifa to JWLA-033 (March 3) listing; the 'JWC adds Eastern Med within 7 days' trigger expires UNTRIPPED tomorrow (May 26) — the cleanest external falsification of the Haifa-blockade rhetoric. The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude (paper dumps tail premium / insurance holds) and to shipping-tankers (rates stabilised $100K/day, volumes -36% NOT unwinding) fired today as a divergence signal.** PRIOR (May 24): **AWRP ~1% HOLDING FROM MAY 19 LEVELS (8x PRE-WAR 0.10-0.15% BASELINE; SOME NO-CLAIMS-BONUS TRANSITS AT 0.8%); CRITICAL ZONE DESIGNATION SINCE MAY 5. INSURANCE MARKET HAS NOT BOUGHT THE DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE: NEITHER THE PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DEAL TEXT (MAY 21-22 LEAK) NOR TRUMP'S 'AGREEMENT LARGELY NEGOTIATED' (MAY 23) NOR THE 60-DAY MOU ARCHITECTURE (MAY 24 AXIOS) HAS PRODUCED AWRP RETRACEMENT. THIS IS THE CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION SIGNAL OF THE CYCLE. HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 6 OF 14 — LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED OR HAIFA TO JWLA-033 (MARCH 3) HIGH-RISK LISTING THROUGH MAY 24. LLOYD'S JWC TRIGGER DAY 6 OF 7 (BY 2026-05-26) — TUESDAY IS THE OPERATIONAL TEST.** Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36%' — 14.4m bpd avg over past eight weeks vs 22.5m prewar. VLCC rates stabilised at ~$100K/day (Baltic US Gulf-China $91,731, West Africa-China $99,407). **The insurance complex's failure to retrace IS the structural intelligence: physical premium is not deal-conditional.** PRIOR (May 23): AWRP ~1% HOLDING FROM MAY 19 LEVELS — INSURERS HAVE NOT BOUGHT THE DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE; PHYSICAL PREMIUM INTACT. IRGC 25-VESSEL HORMUZ CLAIM MAY 23 CONTRADICTED BY INSURANCE-MARKET ABSENCE OF PREMIUM RETRACEMENT.** Per markets dossier F (Lloyd's List / LMA / Insurance Business / Insurance Journal): AWRP ~1% holding (some no-claims-bonus transits at 0.8%); CRITICAL zone designation since 5 May. Hormuz transits ~5-6/day vs historical 138 — IRGC 25-vessel claim CONTRADICTED by insurance market reality. **Houthi Haifa 'maritime blockade' Day 5 of 14**: NO confirmed kinetic attack on Haifa-bound vessel; **Lloyd's JWC has NOT added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to high-risk listing** (JWLA-033 March 3 remains current published listing — no subsequent update through May 23). The 'Lloyd's JWC adds Eastern Med or Haifa within 7 days' trigger remains `watching` Day 5 of 7 (by 2026-05-26) — the cleanest external test of Houthi credibility still uncrossed. **Frontline Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day +75% sequential** (CONFIRMED corporate data via GlobeNewswire) validates the AWRP-elevation read. Lloyd's List May 22: 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer.' Baltic Week 21 VLCC TD3C WS402.5 (-45 pts vs prior Friday); TCE round-trip ~$397,753/day. **Hormuz remains effectively closed** per independent maritime trackers; IRGC 25-vessel claim May 23 likely refers to ALL Hormuz transits including Iranian coastal/cabotage. **1,550 stranded vessels and 22,500 trapped mariners headline figure remains operative.** **The insurance complex's failure to retrace on the deal-anticipation tape is the cleanest paper-physical bifurcation signal of the cycle.** PRIOR (May 22): **MARINE WAR RISK PREMIUM (AWRP) ~1% H&M / 7-DAY — HOLDING FROM MAY 19 LEVELS (8x PRE-WAR 0.10-0.15% BASELINE). INSURERS HAVE NOT BOUGHT THE DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE YET; PHYSICAL PREMIUM INTACT.** Per markets dossier F web-search: AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day holding from May 19 levels (Argus, Lloyd's List). **The insurance complex's failure to retrace on the deal-anticipation tape is the cleanest paper-physical bifurcation signal of May 22**: paper-crude (Brent -5.3%) re-priced the tail premium $7-8 while paper-shipping (Frontline -3.0%, BOAT -2.3%) unwound deal-anticipation positioning — but the insurance complex did NOT retrace. **Frontline Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day +75% sequential** (CONFIRMED corporate data via GlobeNewswire) validates the AWRP-elevation read: the physical premium is structurally elevated and contracted, not speculative. **Houthi Haifa 'maritime blockade' Day 4 of 14**: NO confirmed kinetic attack on Haifa-bound vessel; **Lloyd's JWC has NOT added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to high-risk listing** (JWLA-033 March 3 added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar — no subsequent update). The 'Lloyd's JWC adds Eastern Med or Haifa within 7 days' trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7 — the cleanest external test of Houthi credibility still uncrossed. **VLCC TD3C real-time WS print not surfaced in this window**; relying on Frontline Q1/Q2 TCE data instead. Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day is the operational benchmark; Baltic Exchange daily WS print should be cross-checked next cycle. **Hormuz remains effectively closed** per independent maritime trackers (hormuzstraitmonitor.com, IranWarLive, Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker May 17 'largely frozen'); IRGC claim of 35 vessels in 24 hours May 22 (CLAIMED) likely refers to ALL Hormuz transits including Iranian coastal/cabotage, NOT international VLCC/tanker traffic. **1,550 stranded vessels and 22,500 trapped mariners headline figure remains operative.** PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **MARINE WAR RISK INSURANCE (Persian Gulf): 3-8% of vessel value AWRP ($3-8M/transit); some transits paying 10% per Lloyd's Market Association = 20-50x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline.** Hormuz traffic ~30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war. **Markets dossier E proposes strengthening marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude edge weight to 10.0** (already at 5.0 per yesterday's graph state) — direct mechanism confirmed every day. Reasons: Iranian attacks on tankers + insurer withdrawal of war-risk cover effectively closed strait; LMA: 88% market participants still have appetite for international hull-war underwriting. **VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 ($370-410K/day estimated, ~9x pre-war); Week 19 not yet released through Monday IST.** **B5 VLCC TD3C and AWRP — no fresh data through Monday morning IST**; Lloyd's List 'uncharted territory' coverage carries forward. **Houthi Red Sea Day 7 of 7 negative-space CLOSES UNTRIPPED** today — no second incident since May 11 Hodeidah. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger basis CONFIRMED ($467K Week 17 + WS408 Week 18 partial)**, 5-session sustain pending daily-print verification. **Watch Tuesday-Wednesday for Baltic Exchange Week 19 print** — sustained move above WS500 / >$400K/day = operational confirmation of the Brent-paper move + StanChart $130-145 path. **PRIOR (May 17): MARINE WAR RISK INSURANCE (Persian Gulf): 1.5-3% H&M (some at 10%); ~8-20x pre-war 0.10-0.15% baseline.** Per markets dossier Section F: LMA confirmed cover REMAINS AVAILABLE; **safety, not insurance, driving traffic reduction.** JWC has expanded high-risk zone to entire Persian Gulf. **US/UK/Israeli ships pay ~3x others.** **VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 (~9x pre-war) carries forward as the most recent rate print; Week 19 data NOT yet released through May 17 morning** (Lloyd's List May 11 had quoted 'uncharted territory' $420-462K/day). **No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST**; no second commercial-vessel seizure since May 14 Hui Chuan + Haj Ali. **B4 HOUTHIS NEGATIVE-SPACE DAY 6 OF 7** — no Red Sea attack since May 11 Hodeidah. **Monday May 18 binary**: VLCC TD3C Week 19 release — sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical bifurcation terminal-escalation trigger fires. **If NYT preparation report operationalizes Monday, AWRP doubles immediately.** **PRIOR (May 16):** Marine War Risk (Gulf): 1-5% H&M (some quotes 5-10%); ~8-10x pre-war 0.1-0.15% — JMIC May 4-5: Hormuz Critical; 6 transits May 3 / 5 transits May 4 vs 138 historical. LMA: 88% of underwriters retain Gulf appetite; safety, not availability, halts transit.** Markets dossier H reversibility: AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16 trigger FAILED (still 1-5%). **VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial print shows WS408.13 (~9x pre-war); Week 19 data not yet released through May 16 morning.** Paper-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 has RESOLVED VIOLENTLY into physical confirmation on May 15: Brent +3.3% to $109.26 / WTI +4.2% / WTI +11% weekly. **A8 IRAN ALLOWED 30+ CHINESE SHIPS TO TRANSIT HORMUZ since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols'** — selective transit instrument; non-Iranian non-Chinese transit rate likely lower than headline 30/day. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE (markets dossier E4 carryforward): marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude (CONFIRMED 1st-order edge; AWRP 1-5% H&M direct mechanism in Brent floor).** PRIOR (May 15): **AWRP ~1.0% H&M / 7-DAY (some transits 0.8% post-NCB; 8x pre-war 0.10-0.15%; off March-peak 2.5%); 88% LMA hull underwriters still have appetite; 90% cargo appetite (S&P Global / Strauss Center)**. Some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED VIA BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 17 AT WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** (CONFIRMED — Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report independent operational primary; Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week now corroborated). ~4.2x late-January reading (Week 4 TD3C WS126.5 / $112,394/day) and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. **Week 18 partial shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade')** — still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added on brent-crude is now BASIS CONFIRMED ($467K is far above $200K threshold); 5-session sustain pending daily-print verification.** Hormuz 'CRITICAL' — 5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical. **C1 PARADOX: Brent refusing to fall to $105.68 while VLCC TD3C at $420K = paper-physical bifurcation deepest of cycle.** **C3 FRONTLINE -3.5% 1D / +8.9% 1M / +21.7% 3M — Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply against SPOT VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at $420K+/day**. Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery — investor should treat Frontline equity as leading indicator of de-escalation if it stays sold; treat VLCC TD3C as leading indicator of physical disruption if it stays elevated.** Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. **Container freight (FBX13) China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w) — Mediterranean +15% w/w consistent with Red Sea + Hormuz rerouting persisting.** Tanker-container divergence persists. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M). PRIOR (May 13): **AWRP UNCHANGED AT ~1% H&M / 7-DAY (8x PRE-WAR 0.1-0.15% baseline; off March-peak 2.5% but elevated); 88% LMA hull underwriters still have appetite (Lloyd's List May 6 update).** Trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 — FAILING HARD. **PHYSICAL REALITY REINFORCED — Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** = industry-CEO authority. **VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn at Gulf of Oman without crossing US blockade line = confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs.** Houthi Day 80 Red Sea quiet possibly broken May 11 (UKMTO advisory near Hodeidah; no claim) — if second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days, additional AWRP step-up likely. Second commercial vessel strike inside Qatari EEZ trigger (May 10 + 7 days) Day 2 of 7 NOT TRIPPED. Vessel attribution clarified: Safesea Neha (Marshall Islands, US-managed Safesea Group NJ). Container freight: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity has caught up; tanker market diverging from container market. VLCC TD3C anchored ~$100K/day; Baltic VLCC US Gulf-China $91,731/day; Frontline secured $76,900/day starting 2026 contract; spot premium intact. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE per markets dossier E4: marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude (CONFIRMED 1st-order edge; AWRP ~1% H&M = 8x pre-war is direct mechanism in Brent floor). Edge to be created with initial weight 5-6.** Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. The paper-vs-physical bifurcation continues: Frontline -3.4% 1D on Evercore ISI downgrade (2027-28 oversupply) vs Hormuz 2-5 ships/day physical tightening.

current.cover_pre_crisis: ~0.2-0.25% of hull value (~$25K/year per vessel baseline)
current.cover_current: Gulf AWRP ~1% of hull (some transits 0.8% post-No-Claims-Bonus); ~8x the pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline; headline quotes still run 3-8% = $3-8M per transit. NOT unwinding on the deal/negotiating tape ('Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge' — S&P Global / Khaleej Times, May 28). The May 28 SECOND US defensive strike (4 Iranian drones downed near Hormuz + Iranian ground-control station struck near Bandar Abbas) confirms physical risk is active INSIDE the ceasefire. US/UK/Israeli flags 3x. CRITICAL zone designation since May 5; entire Persian Gulf on Lloyd's JWC high-risk list.
current.multiplier_vs_pre_crisis: ~8x pre-war on the AWRP ~1% (vs 0.1-0.15%); headline 3-8% quotes = 20-50x in extreme transits. Elevated and HOLDING — the live cost channel even as paper oil de-prices; the cleanest paper-physical non-confirmation signal of the cycle (C6). Croft thesis confirmed again by the May 28 US strike on drones/ground-control: insurers will not rush back while Iran still 'manages' passage.

active_triggers.note_2026-05-25: FAILING HARDER. On the strongest deal-anticipation tape of the cycle (Brent <$100, TTF -4.2%, urea -7.4%, India +1.3%), war-risk insurance held 3-8% of hull (8x+ pre-war) and did NOT normalize toward 0.5% — the physical floor is not deal-conditional. Khaleej Times: 'Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge.' Normalization requires actual sustained operational reopening, not a paper deal-anticipation move; physical bleeds out over 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal.

recent_signals.headline: **INSURANCE IS THE LIVE COST CHANNEL EVEN AS PAPER OIL DE-PRICES — GULF AWRP ~1% OF HULL (SOME 0.8% POST-NCB), ~8x PRE-WAR; HEADLINE QUOTES 3-8% = $3-8M/TRANSIT, NOT UNWINDING. THE SECOND US DEFENSIVE STRIKE OF THE CEASEFIRE (4 IRANIAN DRONES DOWNED NEAR HORMUZ + GROUND-CONTROL STATION STRUCK NEAR BANDAR ABBAS, MAY 28) CONFIRMS PHYSICAL RISK IS ACTIVE INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE — CROFT THESIS RE-VALIDATED (C6).** (1) **The confirmation**: today's dominant tape was de-escalatory on paper — the IRGC fired ONE calibrated ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty) and both sides kept negotiating (NOT a return to war) — yet the US still executed a second 'defensive' strike inside the ceasefire: 4 Iranian drones near Hormuz + an Iranian ground-control station near Bandar Abbas. Physical risk (drones, mines, naval harassment) remains live, which is exactly why insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage' (Croft). (2) **The non-confirmation**: paper oil de-priced again on the calibrated/negotiating tape, but Gulf AWRP held ~1% (some 0.8% post-NCB), ~8x the 0.1-0.15% pre-war baseline, with headline quotes still 3-8% = $3-8M/transit — 'Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge' (S&P Global / Khaleej Times). Insurance is the live cost channel even as paper oil de-prices. (3) **Deal hardened around it**: Trump 'no sanctions relief,' nuclear 'deadlock,' WH called Iran's state-TV deal draft a 'complete fabrication,' 60-day MOU awaits Trump's signature — and even on a clean signature the physical cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not a session; if the deal breaks, insurance never moved. (4) **The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude fired again** as a divergence signal: paper de-prices on the calibrated/negotiating tape while insurance holds 8x.
sources: S&P Global / Khaleej Times ('Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge'; Gulf AWRP ~1% of hull / some 0.8% post-NCB / headline 3-8% = $3-8M per transit — markets dossier F)

recent_signals.verification: Gulf AWRP ~1% of hull / some 0.8% post-NCB / ~8x pre-war / headline 3-8% = $3-8M per transit / NOT unwinding = CONFIRMED (S&P Global / Khaleej Times, markets dossier F); second US defensive strike (4 Iranian drones near Hormuz + ground-control station near Bandar Abbas) = CONFIRMED ACTION (intel); IRGC calibrated Kuwait missile intercepted = CONFIRMED ACTION; Croft thesis re-validated (physical risk active inside ceasefire) = CONFIRMED analytical reading; paper-physical non-confirmation = CONFIRMED divergence signal (paper de-priced, insurance held 8x).

recent_signals.headline: **THE PHYSICAL STACK HAS NOT UNWOUND — WAR-RISK INSURANCE STILL ~0.8-1% OF HULL (PEAKS 5%+) vs 0.2% PRE-WAR, NOT UNWINDING; CROFT THESIS VALIDATED IN REAL TIME BY THE MAY 25 STRIKES ON MINE-LAYING BOATS (C6).** (1) **The validation**: the US May 25 self-defense strikes hit two IRGC fast boats that were STILL LAYING MINES in Hormuz (intel A1) — i.e. Iran was laying the very mines the deal requires it to clear, inside the ceasefire. This is the live proof of Croft's 'fine print / insurers won't rush back' thesis: physical risk (mines, SAMs, naval harassment) is ACTIVE even inside the ceasefire, so insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage'. (2) **The bifurcation**: across this ~48h window the PAPER crisis-trade equities bled on deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation — Frontline -3.0% / BOAT -2.0% (tankers), Cheniere -10.3% MoM (LNG), CF -2.1% / Nutrien -4.4% MoM (fertilizer), Lockheed -18.8% 3M (defense), Uranium URA -11.4% MoM — but the PHYSICAL stack priced ZERO operational change: war-risk insurance ~0.8-1% of hull (peaks 5%+) NOT unwinding, VLCC volumes -36% intact, urea >$700/t (+40%), TTF +30% YoY, helium +40-100%. Paper equities are SELLING the deal while physical markets price zero change. (3) **Mechanism**: even on a clean signed deal the physical cost stack (insurance, shipping, gas, fertilizer) bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, NOT a session; if the deal BREAKS (an Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike, intel A3), the paper equities gap back UP first while physical never moved. The physical stack is the SLOW-unwinding floor under any paper relief. (4) **The non-confirmation edge fired today** as a divergence (not co-movement) signal to brent-crude (paper whipsaws/de-prices ~-14% MoM / war premium snapped back +3.58% on the strikes then faded; insurance holds sub-1% but multiples of pre-war) and to shipping-tankers (rates high, volumes -36% NOT unwinding). **Decision-relevant**: the easy crisis-beta paper trade is over; what's intact is the physical stack and the binary on deal signature.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C6, F (war-risk insurance ~0.8-1% of hull, peaks 5%+, vs 0.2% pre-war — Strauss Center / Lloyd's / Caixin; physical stack NOT unwound)

Khaleej Times explicit: 'Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge.' (2) **Mechanism**: insurers and shippers will NOT rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage' — even on a clean signed deal the physical cost stack (insurance, shipping, gas, fertilizer) takes 6-12 weeks to unwind, NOT a session. The equity-relief bounce front-runs the actual economic relief by weeks. War-risk insurance is the SLOW-unwinding physical floor under any paper relief. (3) **Corroborating physical**: VLCC volumes still -36% through Hormuz (Lloyd's List); rates stabilised ~$100K/day; entire Persian Gulf on Lloyd's JWC high-risk list; CRITICAL zone since May 5. (5) **The non-confirmation edge fired today** as a divergence (not co-movement) signal to brent-crude (paper dumps tail premium / insurance holds 8x) and to shipping-tankers (rates stable, volumes -36% NOT unwinding). **Decision-relevant**: even if the deal signs tomorrow, the physical cost stack does not collapse — it bleeds out over a quarter.
sources: Lloyd's / Marsh / Khaleej Times ('Hormuz reopening won't mean cheaper shipping as insurance premiums surge'; war risk 3-8% of hull = $3-8M/transit — markets dossier Section F)
Lloyd's List May 22 (VLCC volumes -36% through Hormuz; rates ~$100K/day stabilized)

Lloyd's Market Association (JWLA-033 March 3 unchanged — no Eastern Med/Haifa addition); Intel dossier 2026-05-25 A8

recent_signals.verification: War-risk insurance 3-8% of hull / NOT unwinding = CONFIRMED (Khaleej Times explicit + Lloyd's / Marsh, markets dossier F); 8x+ vs 0.25% pre-war = CONFIRMED structural math; VLCC volumes -36% = CONFIRMED (Lloyd's List); paper-physical non-confirmation = CONFIRMED divergence signal (paper re-priced the deal, physical did not); Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med/Haifa NOT added = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (JWLA-033 unchanged), trigger on track to expire untripped May 26.

recent_signals.headline: **AWRP HOLDS AT ~1% OF HULL VALUE (DOWN MARGINALLY FROM 2.5% PEAK, SOME 5-10%) ON POSTPONEMENT — STILL 4-6X PRE-CRISIS 0.15-0.25%; ENTIRE PERSIAN GULF ON LLOYDS JWC HIGH-RISK LIST.** AWRP moderating from 2.5% to ~1% per Insurance Business Mag is consistent with postponement-day partial unwind but the structural mechanism remains intact. **Watch list for the Saree-Haifa announcement (CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION)**: if Lloyds JWC adds Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa to its high-risk listing within 7 days, the credibility weight of Houthi reach to Mediterranean is being priced; if no movement, 'rhetoric-only' tag confirmed by the insurance market.

recent_signals.headline: **MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL: Lloyd's List May 11 reports VLCC TD3C 'in unchartered territory' at ~$420-462K/day** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K from markets dossier May 13). **Paper-Brent at $105.68 vs spot VLCC at $420K = paper-physical bifurcation deepest of cycle.** Per markets dossier E proposal: proposed new edge VLCC-TD3C → Brent-Crude at initial weight 9.0; **figure flagged for cross-verification with Baltic Exchange before propagating.** **AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; off March-peak 2.5%); 88% LMA hull appetite, 90% cargo (S&P Global / Strauss Center).** Some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **C3 FRONTLINE -3.5% 1D / +8.9% 1M — Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply** against SPOT VLCC TD3C $420K+/day. Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. Frontline equity SELLING vs tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery. **NEW TRIGGER (added on brent-crude node): VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical divergence terminal escalation.** **Container freight (FBX13): China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w)** — Med rerouting persisting. JWC Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M).
